title: Afrocanthium gilfillanii
taxonomy: Afrocanthium gilfillanii is classified within the Apocynaceae family, which is a family of flowering plants commonly known as the dogbane or oleander family. This classification places it within the order Gentianales. The family Apocynaceae is further divided into several subfamilies, and Afrocanthium gilfillanii belongs to the subfamily Periplocoideae. The genus Afrocanthium is a small group of plants native to tropical Africa, and it contains several species. Within the genus, Afrocanthium gilfillanii is one of the recognized species.
uses: Afrocanthium gilfillanii has been reported to have various medicinal uses in traditional medicine, particularly in the treatment of fever and respiratory issues. The plant's extracts are believed to possess antimicrobial and antimalarial properties, making it a potential natural remedy for certain diseases.
